rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative alkoxysulfonylation of alkenes with sulfonyl hydrazines and alcohols to give beta-alkoxy sulfones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfonyl hydrazine to a sulfonyl radical (via diazo intermediate, losing N2); second anodic oxidation of the beta-sulfonyl carbon radical to a benzyl cation; cathodic reduction of ROH/H+ to H2 and alkoxide

key_intermediates.name: sulfonyl diazo intermediate (R-SO2-N=N*) after anodic oxidation and deprotonation
beta-sulfonyl benzyl carbon radical from addition to styrene
beta-sulfonyl benzyl cation after second anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the sulfonyl hydrazine (with -3H+/-3e-) to a diazo species that loses N2 to give a sulfonyl radical, which adds to the styrene to form a benzyl radical; this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzyl cation that is trapped by the alcohol and deprotonated, while protons and ROH are reduced at the cathode to H2 and alkoxide.

probe_reagents.name: alpha-cyclopropyl styrene (radical clock 2m)
probe_reagents.observation: Both direct beta-alkoxy sulfone 5m (41%) and ring-opened product 5m' (40%) isolated, indicating intermediacy of a carbon radical that undergoes cyclopropyl ring-opening

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of 1a at 1.91 V and 2a at 2.19 V (vs Ag/AgCl), indicating 1a (sulfonyl hydrazine) is oxidized preferentially at the anode

notes: Catalyst-free, additive/oxidant-free electrolysis with carbon anode and Ni cathode in undivided cell; N2 and H2 are the only byproducts. Mechanism proposed on the basis of CV and radical clock evidence; no DFT, TEMPO trapping, or KIE reported.
